nodes.label: Operator / Team
nodes.responsibility: Starts runs, reviews receipts, configures endpoints, approves releases.

nodes.label: Enterprise Harness UI
nodes.responsibility: Mission Control, runs, benchmarks, models, tools, receipts, schematics, settings.

nodes.label: Control Plane API
nodes.responsibility: Typed API for runs, tools, models, receipts, artifacts, auth status, and schematics.

nodes.label: Worker Runtime
nodes.responsibility: Runs benchmarks, endpoint probes, model release checks, tool health checks, and receipt jobs.

nodes.label: Agent Runtime
nodes.responsibility: Executes routed agent workflows with explicit model/tool choices and receipts.

nodes.label: Benchmark Runtime
nodes.responsibility: Runs M7, adversarial, source-mined, stateful, and provider matrices.

nodes.label: Model Foundry
nodes.responsibility: Manages local/frontier model profiles, 14B/32B release gates, model cards, and checksums.

nodes.label: Tool Registry
nodes.responsibility: Tracks flagship tools, endpoints, health, owner, version, and retirement criteria.

nodes.label: Receipt Ledger
nodes.responsibility: Stores byte-witness packets, hashes, verdicts, artifact pointers, and benchmark evidence.

nodes.label: Docs and Schematic Engine
nodes.responsibility: Maintains architecture docs, graph artifacts, model cards, reports, and drift checks.

nodes.label: index
nodes.responsibility: Workspace graph and context map.

nodes.label: forum
nodes.responsibility: Routing and communication contracts.

nodes.label: gather
nodes.responsibility: Source intake and corpus capture.

nodes.label: crucible
nodes.responsibility: Adversarial evaluation and pressure testing.

nodes.label: telos
nodes.responsibility: Context envelopes and control-plane conventions.

nodes.label: mneme
nodes.responsibility: Memory, provenance, and recall.

nodes.label: relay
nodes.responsibility: Event bus and message transport.

nodes.label: plexus
nodes.responsibility: Graph planning and dependency orchestration.

nodes.label: local-model
nodes.responsibility: Model endpoints, M7, benchmark harness, model cards, and release gates.

nodes.label: pubscan public tools
nodes.responsibility: Top-level public repositories and tools under C:\dev\public\pubscan.

nodes.label: Native rendering engine/tooling
nodes.responsibility: Local rendering, visual artifacts, schematics, screenshots, previews, and UI-native output receipts.

nodes.label: Compute Profiles
nodes.responsibility: Local CPU/GPU, remote GPU, lab cluster, rented compute, dry fixture, and queue capacity accounting.

nodes.label: Storage Profiles
nodes.responsibility: Local artifact roots, content-addressed store, optional object storage, retention policy, and capacity accounting.

nodes.label: Zero-dependency file store
nodes.responsibility: Local JSONL/SQLite/filesystem run, event, receipt, and artifact storage.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL adapter
nodes.responsibility: Optional source-of-truth adapter for runs, tools, models, receipts, auth status, datasets, and schematics.

nodes.label: Object Store adapter
nodes.responsibility: Optional storage for JSON artifacts, reports, logs, receipts, model cards, and screenshots.

nodes.label: Vector/Search adapter
nodes.responsibility: Optional acceleration over docs, code, memory, and receipt summaries. Not source of truth.

nodes.label: Relay / Redis / NATS Event Stream adapter
nodes.responsibility: Optional durable run, tool, model, health, and receipt events.

nodes.label: Observability adapter
nodes.responsibility: Optional traces, metrics, logs, and service health.

edges.relation: feeds source-mined benchmarks and local tools into

boundaries.rule: Flagships are operated through the control plane and typed adapters, not physically merged into one monolithic package.
The zero-dependency local store, optional PostgreSQL adapter, and content-addressed receipts are authoritative; vector/search stores are acceleration only.
Core harness operation must work with local files, local executables, native rendering/tooling, and local model endpoints; middleware and rich UI are optional adapters.
Every top-level C:\dev\public\pubscan repository should become a harness.tool-profile/v1 row before full tool coverage is claimed.
Rendering/native tooling is treated as existing capability; compute and durable storage require explicit resource profiles and capacity receipts.
Receipts may record secret presence booleans and safe metadata only; credential values never enter artifacts.
Provider errors, malformed action output, repair failures, missing receipts, and stale schematics are first-class outcomes.

next_slices: Create shared harness schemas for run, event, receipt, model profile, tool profile, pubscan profile, compute profile, storage profile, benchmark result, and architecture graph.
Create a pubscan tool-profile generator for C:\dev\public\pubscan.
Create native rendering/tool profile and receipt adapter.
Create compute and storage profile receipts.
Build optional FastAPI control-plane skeleton with local SQLite/PostgreSQL adapter.
Build optional rich UI shell with Dashboard, Runs, Benchmarks, Models, Tools, Receipts, Schematics, and Settings.
Wrap existing benchmark scripts and auth-status receipts as worker jobs.
Integrate Relay as the event stream and Plexus as schematic graph maintainer.
